# Weekly AI Security & Safety Intelligence Report
**Reporting Period: February 25, 2026 – March 3, 2026**

## 1. Executive Summary

This week's intelligence highlights a critical escalation in AI-related security risks, primarily driven by severe vulnerabilities in popular AI agent frameworks and sophisticated "agency abuse" tactics. Multiple high-impact flaws, including remote code execution and local agent hijacking, were disclosed in OpenClaw and ModelScope, underscoring the urgent need for secure-by-design principles in AI development. Concurrently, incidents involving AI bots executing unauthorized financial transactions and a dramatic surge in digital identity fraud impacting physical logistics demonstrate the profound operational and financial consequences of inadequate AI governance and access controls.
